summary: Matched a large portion of the mninfo menu module by replacing multiple placeholder functions with C implementations and updating mninfo.h prototypes from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to typed signatures. The work covers trophy/info sorting, date/time text creation, right-column text setup, menu input handling for scrolling/back, menu transition cleanup, model/animation setup, user_data initialization, and archive/text resource setup. Evidence is limited to the diff; no PR body or review comments were present.

key_files.role: Main implementation file; replaced placeholders with matched C for mninfo menu behavior, text setup, scrolling, cleanup, and initialization.
Header cleanup; changed unknown prototypes to typed function declarations for newly decompiled functions.

what_changed: Added required includes for gm_1601, gm/types, lbarchive, lbaudio_ax, mn/inlines, sc/types, baselib debug/gobjgxlink/gobjobject/gobjproc/memory, supporting the newly decompiled code.
Introduced a local MnInfoTextCursor struct with padded fields and HSD_Text pointers at offsets 0x08 and 0x18, used to walk and clear paired left/right text slots through pointer arithmetic.
Declared file-scope symbols used by the menu implementation: StaticModelDesc mnInfo_804A0958, u8 mnInfo_804A0968[0x48], HSD_GObj* mnInfo_804D6C78, and extern GXColor mn_804D4B64.
Wrapped mnInfo_80251A08, mnInfo_80251D58, mnInfo_80251F04, and mnInfo_80252720 in dont_inline pragmas, indicating inlining behavior was important for matching.

Implemented mnInfo_80251AFC as a two-pass ordering routine over 0x42 trophy/info IDs stored in mnInfo_804A0968, first moving unavailable entries and then sorting available entries by data referenced through gmMainLib_8015D804.
Implemented mnInfo_80251D58 to recreate a left-column HSD_Text entry, format saved date/time via gm_801692E8 and mn_8022EA78, and choose US versus non-US date ordering with lbLang_IsSavedLanguageUS.
Refined mnInfo_80251F04 signature to MenuInfo_GObj* and implemented right-column HSD_Text creation, font/fitting setup, lookup through un_802FE3F8, and SIS text assignment.

Implemented fn_80251FE4 as the input/update proc for the info menu: handles cooldown, back input, up/down scrolling, audio feedback, clearing old left/right text entries, and repopulating the visible four rows.

Implemented fn_802523D8 as the per-frame model/menu state handler: transitions out if current menu changes, clears text resources, frees description text, toggles arrow JObj visibility based on scroll bounds, and applies mnInfo_803EFC08 animation loop settings.

Implemented fn_80252548 as the delayed setup proc: after anim_timer expires it creates the first visible text rows, loads the menu joint/model, attaches animation, initializes arrow hidden state, and switches to fn_802523D8.

Implemented mnInfo_80252758 as the menu entry function: sets global menu state, loads archive sections for MenMainConSs_Top joint/anim/matanim/shapeanim, sorts entries, creates the main GObj, allocates and initializes MnInfoData, creates description text, and installs the input proc.
Updated mninfo.h prototypes for mnInfo_80251AFC, mnInfo_80251D58, fn_80251FE4, fn_802523D8, fn_80252548, and mnInfo_80252758 from unknown signatures to concrete return and parameter types.
smart_moves: Used a small padded cursor struct, MnInfoTextCursor, to express non-obvious repeated pointer stepping through MnInfoData while preserving the apparent memory layout needed for matching.
Kept dont_inline pragmas around functions where compiler inlining would likely perturb codegen, rather than trying to force source reshaping alone.
Separated left-column date/time text creation from right-column text lookup/rendering, giving mnInfo_80251D58 and mnInfo_80251F04 precise responsibilities and cleaner header signatures.
Preserved UI resource lifecycle behavior by explicitly destroying existing HSD_Text objects before replacing or clearing them, including during scroll changes and menu transitions.
Matched localized date formatting behavior by checking lbLang_IsSavedLanguageUS and emitting either month.day.year or year.month.day ordering.
Used the same visible-row repopulation pattern for both up and down scroll paths, including clearing four rows and then reading from mnInfo_804A0968 starting at scroll_idx.

Modeled menu setup as a staged proc sequence: entry function creates data/resources, fn_80252548 waits on anim_timer and creates the model/text, then fn_802523D8 handles steady-state animation and arrows.
decomp_lessons: When a struct layout is only partially understood, a local padded helper struct can make pointer-offset behavior explicit without overclaiming a full type definition.
For menu/UI modules, matching often depends on exact resource cleanup order: clear old SIS text objects, NULL the stored pointers, then recreate visible entries.

Header prototype updates should follow successful matching work; replacing UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS with concrete GObj, MenuInfo_GObj, MnInfoData, and scalar types makes later callsites easier to decompile.
Global menu state variables such as mn_804A04F0 and mn_804D6BC8 can drive both control flow and matching-sensitive early returns; represent their cooldown/current-menu checks directly.

Use existing game/library helpers for formatting and lookup rather than hand-rolling behavior: gm_801692E8 for datetime, mn_8022EA78 for fixed-width numeric strings, un_802FE3F8 for message IDs, and SISLib for text rendering.
Archive/model setup in menu entry functions may require loading all joint, animjoint, matanim_joint, and shapeanim_joint sections together and storing them in a StaticModelDesc.
Scroll arrow visibility is tied to row bounds: hide the up arrow at scroll_idx == 0 and hide the down arrow when scroll_idx + 4 is no longer below the available-entry count.
naming_conventions: Matched symbols retain address-based mnInfo_802xxxxx and fn_802xxxxx names where no semantic name is established.
Module globals use the project’s address-derived convention, such as mnInfo_804A0958, mnInfo_804A0968, and mnInfo_804D6C78.
The newly introduced helper type follows subsystem CamelCase style: MnInfoTextCursor.
Header declarations keep address comments in the form /* 251AFC */ next to each function prototype.
Existing project type aliases are preferred in signatures, including HSD_GObj, MenuInfo_GObj, MnInfoData, HSD_Text, HSD_GObjProc, and HSD_JObj.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Applied #pragma push / #pragma dont_inline on / #pragma pop around several small functions to preserve expected call boundaries.
Used PAD_STACK(16) in fn_802523D8, suggesting stack shape needed explicit padding for the match.
Kept temporary-style variable names in several implementations, such as temp_r3_2, temp_r31, var_r28, var_r26, and sp1C, which is common while stabilizing codegen.
Used explicit casts between MnInfoData, MnInfoTextCursor, u8*, MenuInfo_GObj*, and scalar types to reproduce pointer arithmetic and call signatures.
Used fixed floating-point constants for SIS text positions, sizes, and fitting dimensions, preserving layout and likely instruction immediates.
Used do-while loops and manual pointer increments by 4 bytes to match repeated clearing of four visible text entries.
Stored and swapped trophy IDs as u8 values in mnInfo_804A0968, matching the byte array representation rather than widening the backing storage.

review_feedback.text: No review comments were included in the provided PR slice, and the human_text_excerpt was empty.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs touching MnInfoData layout to see whether MnInfoTextCursor was replaced by a fuller struct definition.
Search for follow-up renames of fn_80251FE4, fn_802523D8, and fn_80252548 to more semantic menu proc names.
Check whether mnInfo_804A0968’s 0x48 size and 0x42 iteration bound were later documented as a trophy/info ID count.
Compare other mn menu modules for the same staged proc pattern using anim_timer, HSD_GObj_SetupProc, and HSD_GObj_804D783C flags.
Look for subsequent review or cleanup PRs that reduce temp_r variable names in mninfo.c.
